title: Surfaces, Draw Order, Alpha, Transforms, Assets, and Presentation · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent adaptive-arcade team receives a different viewport, device, frame rate, seed, entity count, accessibility profile, platform, and delivery constraint and transfers surfaces, draw order, alpha, transforms, assets, and presentation into a changed owned layered rendering and asset pipeline. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of owned layered rendering and asset pipeline with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit…
objectives: Distinguish display and off-screen surfaces, pixel formats, per-surface and per-pixel alpha, color keys, locks, and conversion timing.
Define clear, background, world, effects, HUD, focus, diagnostics, and presentation order once per frame.
Rotate and scale from an immutable source, recenter output bounds, cache justified variants, and avoid cumulative degradation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Surface Model
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Asteroids team handles case 3943 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to compose draw order, surfaces, alpha, asset conversion, transforms, and one explicit frame presentation; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Surface Model.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish display and off-screen surfaces, pixel formats, per-surface and per-pixel alpha, color keys, locks, and conversion timing. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Surface Model, keep Surface Model connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Render Alternatives
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-render-alternatives-rt3rgc" that uses case 8008 to compose draw order, surfaces, alpha, asset conversion, transforms, and one explicit frame presentation. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Render Alternatives.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Render Alternatives into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Render Alternatives, keep Render Alternatives connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-render-alternatives-rt3rgc
# Competency: asteroids-render-alternatives.
# Case 8008: a keyboard-only community arcade night.
# Operating constraint: retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity.
# Changed case: replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state.
def evidence_asteroids_render_alternatives_rt3rgc(logical_draws: int = 14, presented_frames: int = 14, alpha_assets: int = 4, converted_assets: int = 4, transform_from_original: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_rt3rgc = "rt3rgc-cgr3tr-8008"
    assert evidence_variant_rt3rgc
    evidence_axes_rt3rgc = (
        "playerrt3rgc",
        "seed8008",
        "inputcgr3tr",
        "framert3rgc8008",
        "state8008rt3rgc",
        "playtestcgr3tr8008",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_rt3rgc)) == 6
    if logical_draws < 1 or alpha_assets < 0:
        return False, logical_draws, "render-shape"
    if presented_frames != logical_draws:
        return False, presented_frames, "presentation-drift"
    if converted_assets != alpha_assets:
        return False, converted_assets, "surface-format"
    if not transform_from_original:
        return False, alpha_assets, "destructive-transform"
    assert presented_frames == logical_draws
    return True, converted_assets, "owned-render-pipeline"
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Render Alternatives structural check is only one gate. owned layered rendering and asset pipeline must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Render Alternatives
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Asteroids team handles case 2042 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to compose draw order, surfaces, alpha, asset conversion, transforms, and one explicit frame presentation; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Make ship, hazard, projectile, focus, damage, and status distinguishable without color alone and under reduced effects.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?

options.text: For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof. This does not satisfy Render Alternatives for owned layered rendering and asset pipeline.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Render Alternatives condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

Keep the plausible Render Alternatives output and hide the violated owned layered rendering and asset pipeline assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for Render Alternatives.
